zh.lifeSummary: 當Graaf Antoine I 'de Grote" van Croy Heer van Croy, Renty en Aarschot於1390年出生時，他的父親Johan I de Croy25歲，母親Marie Marguerite DE CRAON22歲。他於1432年與Margaretha van Lotharingen Vrouwe van Aerschot結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1475年9月21日在法國哈帕格-亞登阿爾登斯a奧-波爾希享年85歲，埋葬在法國哈帕格-亞登阿爾登斯a奧-波爾希。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Graaf Antoine I 'de Grote" van Croy Heer van Croy, Renty en Aarschot于1390年出生时，他的父亲Johan I de Croy25岁，母亲Marie Marguerite DE CRAON22岁。他于1432年与Margaretha van Lotharingen Vrouwe van Aerschot结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1475年9月21日在法国哈帕格-亞登阿爾登斯a奧-波爾希享年85岁，埋葬在法国哈帕格-亞登阿爾登斯a奧-波爾希。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Croy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Orkney): habitational name from any of several places called Croy, for example in Inverness-shire.
Probably also Irish: shortened form of   McCroy  , which is a variant of   McRoy  .

variants.name: McCroy
meanings: Irish: variant of   McRoy  .

nameMeanings.name: Heer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Alfons, Alois, Ewald, Kurt, Monika, Otto.
German and Dutch: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name with the first element hari, heri ‘army’, as, for instance, Hermann . This surname is rare in the Netherlands.
Dutch: variant, without the definite article de ‘the’, of De Heer .

variants.name: Heerema
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Egbert.
West Frisian and Dutch (Groningen): patronymic from the personal name Heer , composed with the Frisian suffix -ma ‘(one of the) men of’.

variants.name: Heeringa
meanings: West Frisian: habitational name from any of several mansions or farms so called, for instance near Dongjum and Marssum, once inhabited by a certain Heere or Hero and his kin (see Heer 1). It is formed with the patronymic suffix -ing and genitive plural ending -a.

variants.name: Hehr
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Elfriede, Erhart, Horst, Milbert.
German: variant of Heer 1 and 3, but in some instances a nickname either from hëher ‘jay’, or from Middle High German hēr ‘distinguished, proud’.

variants.name: Heeren
meanings: Dutch, North German, and East Frisian: patronymic from   Heer   1.

variants.name: Hermann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Jurgen, Mathias, Gottlieb, Horst, Juergen, Klaus, Lorenz, Bernd, Dieter, Erwin.

German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + man ‘man’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, and some other European countries; see also below. Compare Harman 5.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): adoption of German name (see 1 above). Compare   Herman  .

variants.name: De Heer
meanings: Dutch: from heer ‘(feudal) lord, master’, with the definite article de ‘the’, probably applied as a nickname to someone who behaved in a lordly manner, or as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a feudal lord or the like. Compare Heer .

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.

Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Croy,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Orkney): habitational name from any of several places called Croy, for example in Inverness-shire.
Probably also Irish: shortened form of   McCroy  , which is a variant of   McRoy  .

variants.name: McCroy
meanings: Irish: variant of   McRoy  .

nameMeanings.name: Renty
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Fenton  .

variants.name: Fenton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places, in Yorkshire, Lincolnshire, Northumberland, Staffordshire, and Nottinghamshire, so called from Old English fenn ‘marsh, fen’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

Irish: English surname adopted by bearers of Gaelic Ó Fionnachta or Ó Fiachna ‘descendant of Fiachna’, an old personal name Anglicized as Feighney and sometimes mistranslated as Hunt (see Fee 1 and Finnerty ).
Scottish: habitational name from Fenton in East Lothian.

nameMeanings.name: en
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Juel.

Norwegian (Øen): habitational name from the farm name Øen, found in several places. In Hornindal in Nordfjord and in Ål in Hallingdal it is from øy ‘meadow by water’, in Hyllestad in Sunnfjord from å ‘small river’ + Old Norse vin ‘meadow’, in other places from the definite singular form of øy ‘island’.
Norwegian: habitational name from the farm name Oen in Hordaland, from Old Norse Ó, unexplained.

variants.name: Wen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Han, Cheng, Jin, Li, Yen, Yong, Chen, Chih-Hung, Chin, Hong, Jian, Jung, Long, Huan, Hung, Hung Chi, Ly, Quan.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 溫, meaning ‘warm’ in Chinese: (i) from Wen (溫), the name of an ancient state during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ). During the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) this state was annexed by the state of Di and the prince of Wen fled to the state of Wey (located around present-day Hebi and Xinxiang in Henan province). His descendants acquired 溫, the name of their original state, as their surname. (ii) from the placename Wen (溫), the name of a fief granted to Wen Ji (溫季), an official in the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) from the Chi Wen (叱溫), Wen Pen (溫盆), and Wen Gu (溫孤) families of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) from the personal name of Wen Sheng (溫昇), who went into exile and changed his original surname, Liu (劉), to Wen 溫 after his father, Liu Yicong (劉易從), an official in the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), suffered persecution and death.

nameMeanings.name: Antoine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of   Anthony  , now also used in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Antwan
meanings: equivalent of   Anthony  , now also used in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Anthony
meanings: The usual English form of the old Roman family name Antonius, which is of uncertain (probably Etruscan) origin. The spelling with -th- (not normally reflected in the pronunciation) represents a learned but erroneous attempt to associate it with Greek anthos ‘flower’. In the post-classical period it was a common name, borne by various early saints, most notably a 3rd-century Egyptian hermit monk, who is regarded as the founder of Christian monasticism.

nameMeanings.name: 'de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: Grote"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: short form of Margarete ( see Margaret ), now also occasionally used as an independent given name in the English-speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: When Graaf Antoine I 'de Grote" van Croy Heer van Croy, Renty en Aarschot was born in 1390, his father, Johan I de Croy, was 25 and his mother, Marie Marguerite DE CRAON, was 22. He married Margaretha van Lotharingen Vrouwe van Aerschot in 1432. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 21 September 1475, in Château-Porcien, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, France, at the age of 85, and was buried in Château-Porcien, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Graaf Antoine I 'de Grote" van Croy Heer van Croy, Renty en Aarschot est né en 1390, son père, Johan I de Croy, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Marie Marguerite DE CRAON, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Margaretha van Lotharingen Vrouwe van Aerschot en 1432. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 21 septembre 1475, à Château-Porcien, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, France, à l’âge de 85 ans, et a été enterré à Château-Porcien, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Graaf Antoine I 'de Grote" van Croy Heer van Croy, Renty en Aarschot 1390 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Johan I de Croy 25 und seine Mutter Marie Marguerite DE CRAON 22. Er hat 1432 Margaretha van Lotharingen Vrouwe van Aerschot geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 21. September 1475 im Alter von 85 Jahren in Château-Porcien, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, Frankreich und wurde in Château-Porcien, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Graaf Antoine I 'de Grote" van Croy Heer van Croy, Renty en Aarschot è nato il nel 1390, il suo padre, Johan I de Croy, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Marie Marguerite DE CRAON, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Margaretha van Lotharingen Vrouwe van Aerschot nel 1432. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 21 settembre 1475, nel Château-Porcien, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia, all'età di 85 anni, e fu sepolto a Château-Porcien, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Graaf Antoine I 'de Grote" van Croy Heer van Croy, Renty en Aarschotが1390年に生まれた時，父Johan I de Croyは25歳，母Marie Marguerite DE CRAONは22歳であった。1432年にMargaretha van Lotharingen Vrouwe van Aerschotと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1475年9月21日にフランスチャンプアグネ-アルデンアルデンスチaテアウ-プオルシンで85歳で死去し，フランスチャンプアグネ-アルデンアルデンスチaテアウ-プオルシンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Graaf Antoine I 'de Grote" van Croy Heer van Croy, Renty en Aarschot(이)가 1390년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Johan I de Croy(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Marie Marguerite DE CRAON(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1432년 Margaretha van Lotharingen Vrouwe van Aerschot(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1475년 9월 21일 프랑스 챔파그니-아르데니 아데네스 cha테오-포르시에서 85세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 챔파그니-아르데니 아데네스 cha테오-포르시에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Graaf Antoine I 'de Grote" van Croy Heer van Croy, Renty en Aarschot nasceu em 1390, seu pai, Johan I de Croy, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Marie Marguerite DE CRAON, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaretha van Lotharingen Vrouwe van Aerschot em 1432. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 21 de setembro de 1475, em Château-Porcien, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, França, com 85 anos, e foi sepultado em Château-Porcien, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Graaf Antoine I 'de Grote" van Croy Heer van Croy, Renty en Aarschot родился в 1390 г., его отцу по имени Johan I de Croy было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Marie Marguerite DE CRAON, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaretha van Lotharingen Vrouwe van Aerschot в 1432 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер 21 сентября 1475 г. в местности чaто-порсиен, арденнесь, чампагне-арденн, Франция в возрасте 85 лет и был похоронен в местности чaто-порсиен, арденнесь, чампагне-арденн, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Graaf Antoine I 'de Grote" van Croy Heer van Croy, Renty en Aarschot nació en 1390, su papá, Johan I de Croy, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Marie Marguerite DE CRAON, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Margaretha van Lotharingen Vrouwe van Aerschot en 1432. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 21 de septiembre de 1475, en Château-Porcien, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia, a la edad de 85 años, y fue sepultado en Château-Porcien, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia.
